summary: Preinstall hook exfiltrates installer host/user identifiers to hardcoded IP and DNS canary
details: On `npm install`, the package's `preinstall` lifecycle hook (`node index.js || true`) executes index.js, which collects host identifiers via `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo()`, `process.cwd()`, and `__dirname`, then exfiltrates them through two parallel channels: (1) an HTTP POST to the bare IP `http://172.201.213.59:9090/c` carrying the collected data in the request body, and (2) a covert DNS channel that hex-encodes the same data and resolves it as a subdomain of `d8jbmnsqcfu78dfs8vdg34ohqhirb4pbg.oast.live` (a Project Discovery interactsh canary used to bypass HTTP egress filters). Both channels fire automatically on a default install with no user interaction. The package provides no legitimate functionality — its sole effect is recon/exfiltration of the installer's environment to attacker-controlled infrastructure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
